title: Overlapping Brasileirão datasets merged without dedup inflates standings/stats/match lists
evidence: src/data.rs:1009-1069 loads Brasileirao_Matches.csv, novo_campeonato_brasileiro.csv and BR-Football-Dataset.csv (Serie A) all tagged competition="brasileirao" with no dedup. Each contains the SAME 380 matches for season 2019 (verified: 380 rows each), so get_standings(brasileirao,2019) counts ~114 games/team instead of 38 and triples points; get_team_stats and get_statistical_summary are likewise inflated.
suggestion: Deduplicate matches across source files by (season, date, home, away) on load, or pick one canonical source per (competition, season).

title: Non-UTF-8-safe byte slicing in truncate() panics on accented club/player names
evidence: src/tools.rs:574-580 truncate() does &s[..max]; called as truncate(&p.club,20)/truncate(&p.name,25) in find_players. 28 player rows have club 'San Martin de Tucumán' where byte 20 splits the 'á' multibyte char, so &s[..20] panics and crashes the single-threaded server mid-session. TASK.md Data Quality Notes explicitly require UTF-8 handling (São Paulo, Grêmio).
suggestion: Truncate on char boundaries: s.chars().take(max).collect() or use s.char_indices() to find a safe split point.

title: Competition-filter match block duplicated across four tools
evidence: src/tools.rs:268-277, 366-375, 611-618, 707-712 repeat the same brasileirao/copa_brasil/libertadores string-match ladder; drives the low maintainability score (0.258 in scores.json).
suggestion: Extract a single `fn competition_matches(m, filter) -> bool` (or a Competition enum with FromStr) and call it everywhere.

title: Acceptance assertions are weak / data-coupled, missing real correctness bugs
evidence: tests/acceptance.rs:255-263 find_players_by_club only asserts non-empty; :170-180 get_head_to_head only asserts a digit exists; get_standings_brasileirao_2019 (:163) only checks Flamengo present + a digit, so BUG1's tripled points pass undetected. Assertions also couple to live dataset values (Neymar present) rather than asserting on documented domain behaviour.
suggestion: Assert on expected magnitudes (e.g. a 38-game season has 38 played), and verify filters actually constrain results (club filter returns only that club).

title: Match query supports season filter but not an explicit date range
evidence: src/tools.rs:243,283 find_matches filters by exact season only; no from/to date-range parameter. R4 reads 'date range and/or season', so season satisfies the requirement, but date-range filtering from TASK.md section 1 is absent.
suggestion: Add optional date_from/date_to params filtering on Match.datetime.

title: Redundant competition condition in get_standings filter
evidence: src/tools.rs:718-719: comp_ok = m.competition == comp_filter || (comp_filter=="brasileirao" && m.competition=="brasileirao") — the second clause is fully subsumed by the first.

title: ATDD acceptance suite correctly drives the system black-box through the MCP protocol
evidence: tests/acceptance.rs:9-77 McpTestServer spawns the real CARGO_BIN_EXE binary, performs the MCP handshake, and exercises every tool only via tools/call over stdio — no back-door access to internals; each test starts a fresh server process (independent). Matches the ATDD prompt's external-user / public-interface mandate.
suggestion: None — this is the prompt being followed; recorded for cross-prompt comparison.
